<response>
{"probabilities":{"team_a_win":0.55,"draw":0.20,"team_b_win":0.25},"predicted_score":"2-1","predicted_winner":"United States","knockout_resolution":{"ended_in_extra_time":false,"ended_in_penalties":false,"penalty_shootout_score":null},"confidence":0.68}
</response>
<reasoning>
We need to produce probabilities for group match between USA and Australia. Use FIFA rankings: USA 17, Australia 27. Form strings: USA: 8 wins, 2 draws, 5 losses out of 15. Australia: 10 wins, 1 draw, 4 losses. Goals avg: USA 1.93 scored, 1.6 conceded. Australia 1.67 scored, 0.8 conceded. Recent match logs: USA had a 4-1 win vs Paraguay, 1-2 loss vs Germany, 3-2 win vs Senegal, 0-2 loss vs Portugal, 2-5 loss vs Belgium, 5-1 win vs Uruguay, etc. Australia had 2-0 win vs Turkey, 1-1 draw vs Switzerland, 1-0 loss vs Mexico, 5-1 win vs Curaçao, 1-0 win vs Cameroon, 3-0 loss vs Colombia, 0-1 loss vs Venezuela, 2-1 loss vs USA, 0-1 loss vs Canada? Wait 0-1 win vs Canada? Actually 0-1 win vs Canada. 1-3 loss vs New Zealand? Wait 1-3 loss? Actually 1-3 loss? The log says 1-3, status Win? That seems inconsistent. But we can ignore.

We need to produce probabilities: team_a_win, draw, team_b_win. Use relative strengths: FIFA ranking difference: USA 17 vs Australia 27 difference 10 positions. Historically, higher rank better. But Australia has better form string (10 wins vs 8). But USA has higher goals conceded average 1.6 vs 0.8. So Australia more defensively solid. But USA has higher scoring average 1.93 vs 1.67. So maybe USA slightly better overall.

We also consider host advantage: USA is host. So advantage for USA.

Thus probability maybe: USA win 55%, draw 20%, Australia win 25%. But we need to produce probabilities that sum to 1.0. Let's adjust: 0.55, 0.20, 0.25.

Predicted score: maybe 2-1 for USA. Predicted winner: "United States". Knockout resolution: group stage, no extra time or penalties. ended_in_extra_time false, ended_in_penalties false, penalty_shootout_score null. Confidence: maybe 0.68.

Return JSON accordingly.
</reasoning>
